summary:
The upper lip develops through the fusion of the maxillary, lateral nasal, and median nasal processes during early embryogenesis. This integration ensures normal morphological structure of both the central and peripheral regions of the upper lip. 

The correct answer is $\boxed{C}$.

Okay, let me now summarize only what was actually reasoned about—no extras beyond core facts directly tied to the query—and keep it strictly two sentences as required.

The upper lip forms via the fusion of the maxillary, lateral nasal, and median nasal processes during embryonic development. All three contributions are essential for establishing the complete anatomical structure of the upper lip.
